# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Continuous Deployment to Production

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy-to-production:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js (example for a Node.js project)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20' # Or your project's required Node.js version

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Or yarn install, composer install, etc.

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build # Or your project's build command

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test # Or your project's test command

      - name: Deploy to Production
        # Replace this with your actual deployment commands.
        # This could involve:
        # - SSHing to a server and pulling/updating code
        # - Deploying to a cloud provider (AWS S3, EC2, Lambda; GCP Cloud Run, App Engine; Azure App Service)
        # - Using a deployment tool (Capistrano, pm2, etc.)
        # - Pushing a Docker image to a registry and updating a service
        # For demonstration, a placeholder message is used.
        run: |
          echo "Deployment initiated for production environment..."
          echo "Deploying application version $(git rev-parse --short HEAD) to production..."
          # Example: Your actual deployment command here
          # For example, using AWS S3 for a static site:
          # aws s3 sync ./build s3://your-production-bucket-name --delete
          # Or using a serverless deploy tool:
          # npx serverless deploy --stage production
          # Or triggering a CI/CD pipeline in another tool
          echo "Deployment to production successful!"
